summary: Added DesignSystem/LocalizationHelpers.swift with a single inlinable helper `L(_ key: String) -> LocalizedStringKey` and routed 130 call sites across 8 files through it via a mechanical Python regex pass. Two phases: (A) 87 Section/Label-literal + Label-String-variable sites; (B) 43 Button-literal sites. Files touched: TodayView.swift, HomeView.swift, WriteView.swift, TrainView.swift, CareerView.swift, BuildInfoView.swift, DigestDetailView.swift, RootTabView.swift, plus the new DesignSystem/LocalizationHelpers.swift and project.pbxproj wiring (A091 build, A191 file reference). One manual revert for a count-display Label that the script over-converted (`Label(L("\(entry.focusThree.count)"))` → `Label("\(entry.focusThree.count)")` and the readinessLabel companion). Build green, all 4 touched-surface domain tests pass, localization parity holds at 19/316/13. Supersedes 7 per-surface follow-up slices (Today/Home/Write/Train-followup/Career/Root/Widgets shell-copy-routing) and the Button-literal-verify slice — all marked done with cross-reference notes. Does NOT handle string-interpolation patterns (`app-localization-string-interpolation-formatters` still queued) or accessibility var-bypasses (`app-localization-accessibility-bypass-audit` still queued). Does NOT make any native-review claim.
validations_passed: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-L-final-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
missing_proof_levels: running-app-smoke for translated content per locale

contract_status_changes.before: The audit slice (commit f80efb9) identified 116 must-fix call sites where SwiftUI's overload resolution selected the StringProtocol path, bypassing Localizable.strings even when the key was present. Of those, 8 had already been fixed by commit 2dfd2d0 (Train + Today/Home Check-in + Write Capture). 108 remained.
contract_status_changes.after: All 130 Section, Label, and Button literal/variable call sites across the 8 listed files now route through `L(_:)` which constructs a `LocalizedStringKey` from a runtime `String`. SwiftUI's LocalizedStringKey overload is now unambiguous because the helper's return type is `LocalizedStringKey` (no longer ambiguous between `String` and `LocalizedStringKey`).
proof: 8 view files now contain L("...") or L(varName) at every Section/Label/Button literal/variable site

residual_risks: The mechanical script's interpolation guard was added after one over-conversion was caught. The reverted `entry.focusThree.count` Label and `readinessLabel` Label now display the runtime String verbatim through Label's StringProtocol overload (acceptable — those are dynamic counts/formatted values, not translation keys). If a future numeric-display literal slips through L() with interpolation, it will look up the interpolated runtime string as a key and silently fall back to the verbatim text — visually correct but semantically wrong.

The L() helper takes a runtime String and constructs LocalizedStringKey. SwiftUI's lookup will then search Localizable.strings for that exact key. Variables that DO carry a valid key (e.g., readinessRow's `label: "Energy"`) work correctly. Variables that hold dynamic strings (computed values, user data) effectively become non-localized but display the dynamic value as-is — same as before the routing, just routed through the localization machinery.

Some visible strings still bypass localization at sites this slice did not touch: `.accessibilityLabel(varString)` cases (10 sites flagged in audit), `Text(varString)` cases where the variable carries a localization key (separate from user content), and interpolated copy like `"Next: \(latest.title)"`. Those remain queued under `app-localization-accessibility-bypass-audit` and `app-localization-string-interpolation-formatters`.
Non-English values for the 130 routed sites remain LLM-drafted. Translation quality has not been validated. `app-localization-native-review-intake` remains blocked.

The L() helper is `@inlinable` and `public`. If the app extension targets (e.g., OwloryWidgets) ever import it, ensure they link the same Owlory module. Today the helper is only used inside the main Owlory target.

If a developer adds a new Section/Label/Button literal call site in the future and forgets to wrap with L(), it may be subject to the same SwiftUI overload bypass. A lint rule or a CI grep check could enforce this; not in scope of this slice.

The SwiftUI behavior pattern is empirical, not formally documented by Apple. Future Xcode/iOS releases could change overload resolution rules such that the L() wrapper becomes unnecessary or, conversely, that more call site classes need it. The fix is conservative — adding L() does not break anything if Apple later prefers LocalizedStringKey by default.
recommended_next_reason: Now that Section/Label/Button literals route correctly, the remaining must-fix class is the ~12 interpolated copy sites ("Next: \(x)", "Active protocol: \(x)", "\(n)/\(m) completed"). Those require presentation formatters and stringsdict-style work, not mechanical regex. After those, the accessibility var-bypass audit can complete the visible-string NLS pass.
